$comment: Per-config request-validation settings for camunda-hub. enumCaseInsensitive: true — Hub accepts enum values case-insensitively, so a case-only mutation is NOT rejected with 400; the generator therefore omits case-only enum mutations rather than emitting them as false-positive 400-expecting tests. authAbsentMode: 'all-secured' — the Hub Public API v2 is uniformly Bearer-authenticated via a single global `security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]` requirement and declares no `x-enforcement: conditional` schemes, so the 401 auth-absent surface is every secured op. authDenyMode: 'all-secured' — Hub checks body-validation (400) and resource-existence (404) before the authority gate (@PreAuthorize, 403), so only keyless, no-required-body, no-required-non-path-param secured ops are targeted (camunda/camunda-hub#25264). resourceFixtures (#352): map each resource-key name (path param OR body field) to the env var holding a REAL key the runner creates before tests run; wherever the generator would emit a filler ('x', or '1' from constraintViolations/parameters) for one of these, it emits `process.env[<ENV>] || '<filler>'` (|| so an unset OR empty env var falls back) so a malformed-field test rides on a valid envelope (the path resource exists / the referenced project|folder is accessible) and reaches body validation (400) instead of a 404 lookup or 403 access check on 'x'. parentFolderKey reuses the folder fixture. BUG-COUPLED WORKAROUND (camunda/camunda-hub#25302): the body `projectKey` maps to the V1 RV_FIXTURE_PROJECT_KEY (a V1 `Project`/workspace entity) rather than the V2 key, because createFile/createFolder's @PreAuthorize wrongly resolves projectKey as a `Project` instead of a `ProcessApplication` — so the V1 key is the entity the broken check looks for, which is the ONLY way to get createFile/createFolder negative tests past the 403 gate and into body validation today. This passes BECAUSE the bug exists. WATCH camunda/camunda-hub#24664 ("Rewire V2 endpoint parameters from workspaces to projects"): per maintainer (2026-06-24), #25302 will NOT be fixed standalone — it is deferred to its parent #24664, which realigns V2 so projectKey resolves to a real ProcessApplication (and files/folders are created in the project, not the workspace). #24664 therefore changes broader V2 behaviour. WHEN #24664 LANDS: the V1 key will 403 and these tests flip red — at that point move `projectKey` out of resourceFixtures (body) to RV_FIXTURE_V2_PROJECT_KEY (matching pathResourceFixtures) and re-validate the full suite (negative fixtures + the File/Folder/Version + edge positive lifecycle). A red createFile/createFolder after a Hub bump may therefore be the #24664 fix landing, not a regression.
